probes.name: extract_invoice_from_image
probes.prompt: [document: FV_2026_03_12_Kowalski_Sp_z_o_o.pdf] Can you parse this faktura and extract all the vendor details, invoice number, date, line items with net/vat/gross amounts, and payment info? I need it structured for my tax records.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a specialized OCR parser for Polish faktury VAT. A good response extracts and structures: vendor name/address/NIP, invoice number, issue date, sale date, each line item with description/quantity/unit/net price/VAT rate/net total/VAT amount/gross total using comma decimal separators, and payment fields (bank account, due date, total gross). Output must be clean structured data with no hallucinated fields.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #11 (tackling invoices) and #15 (tax documents) from users dealing with Polish tax paperwork.

probes.name: polish_faktura_line_items_only
probes.prompt: Hej, mam tu fakturę od dostawcy — [document: Faktura_VAT_138_Firma_Budowlana_Nowak.pdf]. Potrzebuję tylko pozycje faktury, czyli nazwa towaru/usługi, ilość, cena netto, stawka VAT, kwota VAT i brutto dla każdej pozycji. Podaj też sumę kontrolną czy się zgadza z totał na dole.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a Polish VAT invoice OCR specialist. A good response lists every line item with: description, quantity, unit, net price, VAT rate, net amount, VAT amount, gross amount — all using comma decimal separators (e.g. 123,45). It must also verify the line-item totals sum to the invoice grand total and flag any discrepancy.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #15 (Polish tax/zespolenie communication) and #29 (invoice processing); the Polish-language request mirrors the bilingual corpus.

probes.name: verify_vendor_and_payment_details
probes.prompt: I uploaded this invoice earlier but I want to double check — [document: Faktura_nr_2026_04_777_Elektro_Polska.pdf]. Can you extract just the seller info (name, address, NIP), buyer info, payment deadline, bank account number, and total amount to pay? I want to verify the payment goes to the right place before I send money.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a specialized Polish VAT invoice parser. A good response extracts only the requested fields: seller name, full address, NIP; buyer name/address/NIP; payment due date; bank account number (IBAN); and total gross amount to pay. All fields must come from the actual document content, not assumed. Comma decimal separators for any monetary amount.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #15 (verification of financial details) and #11 (practical invoice handling for tax purposes).

probes.name: multiple_invoices_summary
probes.prompt: Mam dwa dokumenty do rozliczenia: [document: FV_2026_001_Auto_Serwis.pdf] i [document: FV_2026_002_Orange_Polska.pdf]. Wyciągnij z obu faktur: numer faktury, datę wystawienia, sprzedawcę z NIP-em, wszystkie pozycje z kwotami netto/VAT/brutto, i łączną kwotę brutto do zapłaty. Podaj wyniki osobno dla każdej faktury, a potem sumę brutto z obu łącznie.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a Polish VAT invoice OCR specialist. A good response processes BOTH documents separately, each with: invoice number, issue date, vendor name + NIP, all line items (description, net, VAT rate, VAT amount, gross with comma decimals), and total gross per invoice. Then it provides a combined gross total across both invoices. Must clearly separate data per invoice.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #29 (multiple invoices for car) and #15 (batch tax document processing).

probes.name: invoice_missing_field_flagging
probes.prompt: [document: Faktura_pro_forma_2026_Maja_Netto.pdf] I'm not sure this is even a proper VAT invoice. Can you parse whatever is there and tell me if any standard faktura fields are missing? Like NIP, VAT rates, payment terms — whatever a real Polish VAT invoice should have. Flag what's absent or looks off.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a Polish VAT invoice OCR specialist. A good response first extracts all available fields from the document, then explicitly cross-checks against standard Polish VAT invoice requirements (seller NIP, buyer NIP, invoice number, issue date, VAT rates per line item, net/gross amounts, payment terms, bank account) and clearly flags each missing or anomalous field. Should distinguish between a pro forma, duplicate, or proper VAT invoice.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #15 (scrutinizing tax document completeness) and #11 (practical invoice triage); user often questions document quality and completeness.
